species.species_source: d&d basic rulles 5e, mod by smash

lore.title: Description
lore.text: Thieving claws are the severed hands of thieves animated by dark magic so that they can go on steeling. The Idle Hands Cult have been known to love the act thieving and collecting wealth that they use thieving claws as extra hands in their labors.

lore.title: Magical Origins
lore.text:
Through dark necromantic rituals, the life force of a thief is bound to its severed hand, haunting and animating it. If a dead theif's spirit already manifests as another undead creature, if the thief is raised from death, or if the spirit has long passed on to another plane, the ritual fails. [
]The ritual invoked to create a thieving claw works best with a hand recently severed from a thief. To this end, ritualists and their servants frequent cut off their own hands

lore.title: Creator’s Control
lore.text:
A thieving claw can’t be turned, nor can it be controlled by spells that control undead. These foul monsters are entirely bound to the will of their creator, which can concentrate on a claw in sight to mentally command its every action. If the crawling claw’s creator doesn’t command it, the claw follows its last command to the best of its ability. [
]Commands given to a crawling claw must be simple. A claw can’t be tasked with finding and killing a particular person, because its limited senses and intelligence prevent it from tracking and picking out specific individuals. However, a command to kill all creatures in a particular locale works. A crawling claw can easily feel out the contours of keys and doorknobs, crawling from room to room on a blind killing spree.

lore.title: Malign Intelligence
lore.text: A thieving claw possesses little of the intellect and memories of the individual of which it was once a living part. The joy of taking, steeling, collecting, and greed that drove that person to steal lingers on.  A zombie needs to eat brains, just as the thieving claw needs to take from others. Left to its own devices, a crawling claw imitates and recreates the same thieving acts it committed in life.

lore.title: Living Claws
lore.text:
If a thieving claw is animated from the severed hand of a still-living klepto, the ritual binds the claw to the thief’s soul. The disembodied hand can then return to its former limb, its undead flesh knitting to the living arm from which it was severed. [
]Made whole again, the thief acts as though the hand had never been severed and the ritual had never taken place. When the thieving claw separates again, the living body falls into a coma. Destroying the crawling claw while it is away from the body kills the thief. However, killing the thief has no effect on the crawling claw.

lore.title: Undead Nature
lore.text: A thieving claw doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

special_abilities.title: turn immunity
special_abilities.description: The claw is immune to effects that turn undead.

special_abilities.title: klepto perks
special_abilities.description: Thieving claws get advantages on all sleight-of-hand checks.

actions.title: claw
actions.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) bludgeoning or slashing damage (claw’s choice).
